en: Well, then getting back to the horses, the value they had for the horses and the horse riding skill they developed throughout their life created one of the most unstoppable military forces in history.
ko: 아마 물리학뿐 아니라 모든 분야에 또 다른 일반 상대성이론이 있을 거예요.

en: So if we just talk about the mounted archery that they've employed in war, the Mongols were able to do targeted shooting accurately at 200 meters or more while riding fast, you know, up to speeds of 60 kilometers an hour, I read.
ko: 아, 여기 방 안의 코끼리를 설명했구나 싶은 이론이 있을 거예요. 아무도 생각 안 한 거요.

en: So there's a lot to say, like, you know, you have to time and just watching some of the videos, it's just incredible how stable you can be on top of a horse.
ko: 물리학에서 알려진 것만 해도, 지구 생명의 기원에 그런 게 있을 수 있어요.

en: And I guess you're supposed to be shooting at a moment of the gallop when all four of the feet of the horse are off the ground, and so you have to time all of that, you have to position your body to maintain balance, and then there's the skill of the actual holding and shooting the bow accurately.
ko: 다들 그래, 그래 하면서 어떻게든 시작됐다고 하죠. 아무도 모르잖아요. 그게 이렇게 수수께끼인 게 놀라워요.

en: And there's obviously the technology of the bow, the composite bow, the recurve bow. They've also, I read, used crossbows later, they've adapted the technology.
ko: 이상하죠. 실험실에서 재현이 안 돼요. 일반 상대성이론급 뭔가가 있을 거예요.

en: And there's a particular kind of thumb draw that you use for shooting with the composite bow that works for a horse, because the thing is bouncing up and down, right?
ko: 외계인이나 웜홀이나 우리가 이해 못 하는 차원이 관련될 수도 있어요. 아닐 수도 있고.

en: So you have to, like, not drop the arrow. It's just incredible to be able to shoot while the horse is going 60 kilometers an hour.
ko: 화학이나 생물학과는 근본적으로 다른 느낌의 분야일 수 있어요.

en: Anyway, can you speak to this kind of exceptional excellence that the Genghis Khan and the Mongols had for riding horses and engaging in war off of the horse?
ko: 물리학을 통해서일 수도 있어요. 생명의 기원의 열쇠가 물리학에 있을지도요.

en: The Mongol, the horse, and the bow were a perfect combination, and it was the most lethal weapon known to the world before the modern era.
ko: 그리고 이상한 이웃 같은 게 의식이에요.

en: It was incredible, the synchronization and the timing of the movements, and also the years of skill, the fact that from absolute birth, the Mongols would be on a horse, and by three years old, they would probably be riding alone on the horse.
ko: 이상한 이웃. 맞아요. 생명이 어떻게든 지구에서 시작된 건 다 알아요.

en: Now, when I first went to Mongolia in the 1990s, at that time, all jockeys on horses for races had to be under six years old.
ko: 아무도 방법을 몰라요. 우리가 의식이 있다는 것도 다 알아요. 주관적 경험이 있다는 거요.

en: That was the age limit, the cutoff was six years old at that time. And so you had someone who's three years old racing out there.
ko: 아무도 그걸 이해 못 해요.

en: It's absolutely incredible. And of course, at that age, they can't even have a saddle because it can't even be used.
ko: 사람들이 아이디어가 있지만 어두운 방에 들어가서 속삭이는 거예요. 이 방에 뭐가 있지?

en: So they're just, all they're doing is staying on the horse. The horse has been trained to do what it has to do, and they just stay on it.
ko: 아무도 모르는데 누군가 일반 상대성이론급 개념을 가져오면 모든 걸 재구성하고 아하 하는 거예요.

en: But by staying on it, they learn the horse, they become one, and not just one horse with one rider, but one rider with several horses.
ko: 분수령 같은 순간이죠. 그래요. 그런 이론이 나올 때까지 우리는 이 시대에 살고 있어요.

en: They usually, five is the number that you should have for you when you go off to battle. And this ability, they didn't shoot.
ko: 나중에 보면 당연할 텐데 지금은 모르죠.

en: You have to defend your animals. There are wolves around, foxes, other things, and in some areas, there were even tigers and other animals that would come in.
ko: 시공간이 휘어진다는 건 아무도 생각 못 했지만 뉴턴도 뭔가 이상하다고 알았어요.

en: And you had to be able to shoot to defend it against other people who might be raiding you. So they became excellent archers.
ko: 뭔가 빠져있다는 걸 알았어요. 우리 아이디어를 시험하는 상황이 항상 매력적이에요.

en: They had composite bows that were very powerful, much more powerful than those of most sedentary people.
ko: 뉴턴이 중력 이론으로 한 건 놀라워요. 태양 주위 지구와 나무에서 떨어지는 사과가 같은 현상이라는 거요.

en: Now, I say all that because it's very important, but those are all sort of nomadic traits of the Great Steppe anyway.
ko: 미친 거죠. 엄청난 도약이에요. 관성 질량과 중력 질량이 같다는 거요.

en: I mean, in an earlier version, you had the Huns who came out of Mongolia, and Hun is just the Mongolian word for human, Hun, that's, to this day, that's what they say for a human being.
ko: 믿을 수 없는 도약. 완전한 천재. 하지만 지구가 안 만지는데 사과가 떨어지는 게 싫었어요.

en: So they came out of Mongolia, and all the early Turkic groups came out of Mongolia, and they had the similar skills.
ko: 원격 작용 문제요. 정말 이상하죠.

en: So you have this perfect weapon, but also you have to have perfect strategy in how to coordinate it and organize it and use it.
ko: 정말 이상해요. 하지만 아인슈타인이 풀었어요. 지구가 공간의 곡선을 만들고 사과는 자유낙하하고 싶은 거예요.

en: And this is where the genius, that I cannot explain at all, but the genius of Genghis Khan came in.
ko: 나무가 방해하는 거죠. 나무가 사과를 가속시키고 있어요. 중력장에서 무중력인 자연 상태에서 멀어지게요.

en: Other people, I think, had been very good in earlier times, a number of Turkic leaders, and also, or even Attila the Hun, who, of course, was actually born in the West, but they were charismatic leaders and very dramatic leaders, and it wasn't that they were so excellent in their strategy.
ko: 나무가 놓으면 사과는 그냥 곡선을 따라 떨어져요.

en: They were very good in warfare, and that's what carried them through. Genghis Khan's army was extremely good in warfare, but small.
ko: 뉴턴 시대로 돌아가서 이걸 말해주면 좋겠다. 아마 히피가 중력은 시공간의 곡률이야라고 할 거예요.

en: He never got probably above 100,000, at the most, 110,000.
ko: 모든 아이디어에는 때가 있어요.

en: That is small. When you're going against China, that has millions just in the army, not to count in the country, and you're going against Russia, and you're going against the Middle East and Persia and Afghanistan, and these areas, your whole army has to be as finely tuned as each rider, each bow, and each horse.
ko: 이해하지 못할 수도 있어요. 가장 위대한 천재도 때로는 맥락이 너무 달라서요.

en: That's the weapon, what the army becomes the super weapon of Genghis Khan, and how he organized it and how he used it, and the strategies that he put together.
ko: 거인의 어깨 위에, 그 거인의 어깨 위에 서야 해요.

en: Yeah, when you have a small army, just think about that. A small army that conquered the world.
ko: 뉴턴이 그 말을 경쟁자 후크에 대한 불친절한 비꼼으로 썼다고 들었어요.

en: It would fit in a stadium today in America.
ko: 그때도 뒷담화를 했네요. 트래시토킹. 좋다.

en: So there's extreme efficient coordination of units, mostly cavalry, right?
ko: 인간의 재미있는 면이에요. 과학자들도 거대한 두뇌를 가진 사람들도요.

en: All cavalry. Oh, it's all cavalry. He had no infantry, and he had no baggage train.
ko: 역사에서 대학에서도 어디서든 이런 순간이 있어요.

en: He had no backup commissary. Early on, no engineer corps.
ko: 거대한 두뇌에 에고까지 합치면 어디서나 벌어지는 똑같은 막장 드라마예요.

en: Later, one was added much later.
ko: 가장 큰 우주적 대상과 힘에 대해 생각하면서도 여전히 질투하죠.
